title: Relapsed AML with mutated TP53 after second alloSCT and invasive fungal pneumonia in a 27-year-old female

patient.chief_complaint: Worsening cytopenia, dyspnea, and pleuritic chest pain in a post-alloSCT patient
patient.vital_signs: BP 105/70 mmHg, HR 110/min, RR 18/min, T° 37.9 °C, SpO₂ 91% on room air.

1.setting: Acute hematology / transplant follow-up (post-alloSCT with new respiratory symptoms)
1.capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically fragile, immunocompromised patient after allogeneic stem cell transplantation who presents with new respiratory symptoms and cytopenias. You can obtain a focused history and examination, request routine laboratory testing, and arrange appropriate non-invasive imaging and specialist assessments. Invasive procedures and systemic treatment decisions are guided by these initial findings and the patient’s clinical stability.

2.setting: Inpatient hematology ward (relapse workup and infectious evaluation after alloSCT)
2.capabilities: The patient is admitted for clarification of suspected relapse and concurrent infection. You can organize bone marrow assessment, extended laboratory and microbiologic workup, advanced imaging, and multidisciplinary consultations. Some specialized molecular or cytogenetic tests may still be pending.

3.capabilities: You now have access to bone marrow morphology and flow cytometry, full cytogenetics and molecular profiling, detailed infectious workup, and a complete longitudinal treatment history including two allogeneic transplants and prior FLT3 inhibitor therapy. You are expected to integrate these data to define the relapse type and risk, and to formulate a realistic treatment and supportive care plan that addresses both leukemia and active infections.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics, comorbidities, social background, or transplant history.
demographics.content:
Age: 27 years
Sex: Female
Performance status: ECOG 0
Comorbidities: History of fungal pneumonia, hypothyroidism, secondary hypertension. History of alloSCT for MDS.
Social: Lives with partner and one child (2 years old). Parents alive. Two siblings (sister 31 years, brother 24 years).

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, physical examination findings, or current medication list.
history_physical.content:
2025-01-30: The patient presented with worsening cytopenia, dyspnea, and pleuritic chest pain. She reported low-grade fevers and cough for one week, with one episode of blood-tinged sputum. Physical examination: Fatigued, pale, and mildly tachypneic. No signs of acute graft-versus-host disease: skin without rash, no oral ulcers, abdomen soft and non-tender, no diarrhea, and no hepatobiliary tenderness. Lungs with crackles and pleuritic pain over the left base. Irregular heart rhythm without murmurs. Vitals: BP 105/70 mmHg, HR 110/min, RR 18/min, T° 37.9 °C, SpO₂ 91% on room air.
Current medication: Valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, posaconazole 300 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 960 mg 3×/week, ramipril 2.5 mg 1-0-0, mycophenolate mofetil 360 mg 1-0-1, letermovir 480 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IU twice weekly, levothyroxine 125 µg 1-0-0, folic acid 5 mg daily, ursodeoxycholic acid 250 mg 3×/day.

cbc_chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for complete blood count, differential, coagulation, and serum chemistry.
cbc_chemistry.content:
CBC: Hb 9.7 g/dL, WBC 1.8 × 10⁹/L, Plt 42 × 10⁹/L; ANC 0.3´8 × 10⁹/L, lymphocytes 0.4 × 10⁹/L, monocytes 0.5 × 10⁹/L. No peripheral blasts.
Chemistry: Na 133 mmol/L, K 4.9 mmol/L, creatinine 1.6 mg/dL, BUN 25 mg/dL, bilirubin 1.9 mg/dL, AST 144 U/L, ALT 152 U/L, GGT 165 U/L, ALP 140 U/L, albumin 2.9 g/dL, LDH 568 U/L, CRP 9.7 mg/dL, procalcitonin 0.6 ng/mL, INR 1.5, aPTT 42 sec.

abdominal_ultrasound.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound or sonography of liver, spleen, and kidneys.
abdominal_ultrasound.content: Abdominal ultrasound: Hepatomegaly (17.5 cm), mild splenomegaly (13 cm). Kidneys normal in size and cortical thickness. Gallbladder with mild wall thickening, no Murphy’s sign, and no biliary obstruction. Bowel with mild mural thickening without obstruction.

ct_thorax_pns.valid_request_if: Requests for CT imaging of the thorax and paranasal sinuses.
ct_thorax_pns.content: CT thorax/PNH: Progression of a left-sided pleura-near fungal lesion (3.2 cm, cavitary with halo sign) and several smaller bilateral nodules. Mediastinum without lymphadenopathy. Chronic sinusitis with mucosal thickening in the ethmoidal and maxillary sinuses.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, and histology.
bone_marrow.content:
Cytology: Hypocellular marrow (cellularity 25%). Megakaryopoiesis markedly reduced, consisting mainly of juvenile forms with nuclear budding and fragmentation. The erythropoiesis-to-granulopoiesis ratio is shifted toward erythropoiesis, which displays dysplastic features with frequent mitotic figures. Granulopoiesis shows toxic granulation and matures to segmented forms. Increased numbers of macrophages and plasma cells are present but not expanded. Approximately 10% blasts with deeply basophilic cytoplasm, prominent nucleoli, and occasional Auer rods.
Flow cytometry: B-cell compartment depleted, relative increase in NK/T cells, and reduced granulocytes. No plasma cell expansion. Blasts comprise 13% of nucleated cells, positive for CD34, CD117, CD123 HLA-DR, CD13, and negative for CD33, CD7 and CD56.
Histology: Hypocellular marrow with dysplastic changes in all hematopoietic lineages. Reticulin staining shows focal fibrosis (MF-1). CD34 immunohistochemistry demonstrates focal infiltration by blasts up to 15%.

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for conventional cytogenetic analysis or karyotype.

extended_diagnostics.valid_request_if: Requests for extended infectious workup, BAL results, cardiac and pulmonary function assessment, and iron/vitamin status.
extended_diagnostics.content:
Peripheral virus PCR: CMV negative, EBV negative, adenovirus detected (CT 17). Serum β-D-glucan negative, galactomannan positive (1.7). BAL performed on 01/31/25: Pseudomonas aeruginosa (resistant to cephalosporins, penicillins, and fluoroquinolones) and Aspergillus fumigatus detected in culture. Adenovirus-PCR positive. Galactomannan 2.1.
Echocardiography: Normal left ventricular size and function, LVEF 62%, no wall motion abnormalities, no significant valvular disease, no pericardial effusion.
ECG: Sinus rhythm 86/min, QTc 460 ms.
Pulmonary function tests: FVC 2.3 L (65% predicted), FEV1 1.7 L (58% predicted), DLCO 55% predicted.
Blood cultures: Negative (multiple sets).
Vitamins: Folic acid 13 ng/mL, vitamin B12 890 pg/mL.
Ferritin: 6800 µg/L.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for next-generation sequencing or mutation profiling.
molecular_panel.content: FLT3-ITD ratio 0.031, DNMT3A (VAF 24%), TP53 mutation (VAF 12%). No mutations detected in ASXL1, RUNX1, BCOR, CEBPA, DDX41, IDH1, IDH2, NPM1, SRSF2, STAG2, U2AF1, ZRSR2, or MECOM. No KMT2-, MECOM- or NUP98-rearrangements identified.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease status and markers to track.
mrd_status.content: Active disease, LAIP: CD34, CD117, CD123, HLA-DR, CD13. DNMT3A; FLT3-ITD, TP53 Mutations.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior MDS/AML diagnosis, transplant history, and all systemic therapies.
treatment_history.content:
2023-02: Diagnosis of myelodysplastic neoplasm with low blasts (MDS-LB, WHO 2022) harboring a DNMT3A mutation (VAF 52%). IPSS-M: moderate-high risk; IPSS-R: high risk. The patient became transfusion-dependent for RBCs and platelets (every 1-2 weeks). Donor search initiated with upfront allogeneic stem cell transplantation (alloSCT) planned.
2023-03-22: Transformation to secondary AML with DNMT3A (VAF 12%), and FLT3-ITD (ratio 0.16). Bone marrow blasts 26%, LDH 874 IU/L.
Induction chemotherapy: daunorubicin 60 mg/m² i.v. days 1–3, cytarabine 200 mg/m² continuous infusion days 1–7, plus midostaurin 50 mg p.o. BID days 8–21.Response: 2023-04-25: Refractory disease with 17% bone marrow blasts.
2023-05-09: Salvage and conditioning with FLAMSA-RIC (fludarabine 30 mg/m² days –12 to –9, amsacrine 100 mg/m² days –12 to –10, cytarabine 2 g/m² BID days –12 to –9), followed by 4 Gy TBI daily until day –3.
2023-05-22: Allogeneic stem cell transplantation (alloSCT) from an HLA-matched unrelated male donor (both B Rh+, CMV pos/pos).
Engraftment: leukocytes >1.0 ×10⁹/L on 2023-06-11; platelets >20 ×10⁹/L on 2023-06-14.
Bone marrow (2023-06-15): CRi; FLT3-ITD 0.003, DNMT3A negative; recipient chimerism 0.21%.
2023-06: Cutaneous aGvHD grade I (décolleté). 2023-07-01: Progression to grade I–II (scalp, neck); treated with topical and subsequently systemic steroids (prednisolone 1 mg/kg from 2023-07-05).
2023-07-07: Skin aGvHD grade III → prednisolone escalated to 2 mg/kg; ruxolitinib added (2023-07-16).
2023-08-29: Bone marrow: DNMT3A 3%, FLT3-ITD 0.091, chimerism 1.7% recipient. Started gilteritinib 120 mg daily (2023-09-02) & ruxolitinib was tapered and stopped.
2023-09-12: Bone marrow: FLT3-ITD 0.344, no blasts; chimerism 3.5% recipient. Gilteritinib increased to 160mg/d, donor lymphocyte infusions planned.
2023-09-29: Bone marrow and histology: no blasts, FLT3-ITD 0.14, chimerism 0.26%.
2023-11-05: Bone marrow: FLT3-ITD negative, chimerism <0.06% recipient. DLI collected from donor.
2023-10: Flare of cutaneous GvHD grade III → treated with ECP, re-initiation of ruxolitinib and prednisolone 2 mg/kg with taper.
2024-01-23: Bone marrow: molecular CR, chimerism 0.08% recipient.
2024-04-03: Relapse with 17% bone marrow blasts, new complex karyotype.
2024-04-12: Salvage therapy with venetoclax + HAM (high-dose cytarabine 1.5 g/m² BID days 1–3, mitoxantrone 10 mg/m² days 3–5, venetoclax ramp-up to 70 mg daily due to posaconazole prophylaxis). Response: MLFS; FLT3-ITD and DNMT3A negative; 46,XY karyotype; full donor chimerism.
2024-05-15: Donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) 1 × 10⁶ CD3⁺/kg → complicated by acute grade IV GI GvHD. Treatment: high-dose prednisolone → ruxolitinib 10 mg BID → mycophenolate 1 g BID i.v. → tacrolimus 2 mg/day. Vedolizumab 300 mg q14d added with gradual improvement. No further DLI were given. Alternative donor search was initiated.
2024-07-24: Bone marrow: FLT3-ITD negative, chimerism 0.9% recipient, complex karyotype in 2/20 metaphases.
2024-08-20: Second alloSCT from haploidentical sibling donor after conditioning with fludarabine 30 mg/m² days –6 to –3, treosulfan 14 g/m² days –6 to –4, thiotepa 5 mg/kg day –7, and post-transplant cyclophosphamide 50 mg/kg days +3 and +4.
Post-second SCT complications: Bloodstream infection with ESBL-producing Pseudomonas aeruginosa and septic shock, treated with cefiderocol 2 g q8h. HSV stomatitis with viremia → acyclovir then foscarnet. Virus pneumonia (high HSV copies from BAL) → ICU admission, intubation, ARDS requiring VA-ECMO. Coombs-positive hemolysis → 4 doses rituximab 375 mg/m². Acute renal failure (creatinine 3.1 mg/dL) requiring temporary dialysis. Patient was extubated on 2024-09-20.
Engraftment with leukocytes >1.0 x10⁹/L occurred on 2024-09-21 (day +32) with G-CSF support; platelets >20 x 10⁹/L on 2024-10-01 (day +42).
2024-10-12: Bone marrow: CRi, molecularly negative, chimerism <0.06%, 46,XY.
2024-12-12: Start quizartinib maintenance 26.5 mg daily.
2024-12-28: ECG showed QTc 512 ms and blood counts decreased → quizartinib maintenance was discontinued.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise statement of current relapse type.
disease_status.content: Relapse of AML with clonal evolution, marked by emergence of an additional TP53 mutation (2025/01) and complex karyotype (2024/04).

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for contextual factors relevant to prognosis and treatment choice, including infections, prior FLT3 inhibitor exposure, and organ function.
special_considerations.content: Relapse of AML with clonal evolution, accompanied by active bacterial & fungal pneumonia, adenovirus infection. The patient has failed multiple FLT3-inhibitor therapies. Currently no evidence of active GvHD. Bone marrow reserve is fragile, but overall organ function has recovered, with preserved cardiac ejection fraction and stable renal parameters. Performance status remains good (ECOG 0).

round_1.working_diagnosis: Suspected MDS/AML relapse after alloSCT with concurrent pulmonary infection
differentials: Invasive pulmonary aspergillosis with bacterial superinfection

treatment_plan.action: Perform bone marrow aspirate and biopsy to evaluate for hematologic relapse
Obtain broad infection cultures (blood, urine, swabs) and CMV/EBV diagnostics
Perform bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) for fungal, bacterial, and viral pathogens
Initiate broad-spectrum IV antibiotics for febrile neutropenia
Class switch antifungal treatment to liposomal amphotericin B given progression of fungal lesion on azole prophylaxis

key_findings_quotes: The patient presented with worsening cytopenia, dyspnea, and pleuritic chest pain.
She reported low-grade fevers and cough for one week, with one episode of blood-tinged sputum.
History of alloSCT for MDS, ANC 0.38 x 10^9/L
CBC: Hb 9.7 g/dL, WBC 1.8 x 10^9/L, Plt 42 x 10^9/L
CT thorax/PNH: Progression of a left-sided pleura-near fungal lesion (3.2 cm, cavitary with halo sign) and several smaller bilateral nodules.
T 37.9 C, SpO2 91% on room air

round_2.working_diagnosis: AML post-chemotherapy with complex karyotype after alloSCT

treatment_plan.action: Initiate antiviral treatment for adenovirus infection
Adjust IV antibiotics based on BAL results showing multidrug-resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa
Await molecular panel results to complete risk stratification before initiating leukemia-directed therapy
Monitor organ function and cardiopulmonary reserve for therapy eligibility assessment

key_findings_quotes: Approximately 10% blasts with deeply basophilic cytoplasm, prominent nucleoli, and occasional Auer rods.
Blasts comprise 13% of nucleated cells, positive for CD34, CD117, CD123 HLA-DR, CD13, and negative for CD33, CD7 and CD56.
BAL: Pseudomonas aeruginosa (resistant to cephalosporins, penicillins, and fluoroquinolones) and Aspergillus fumigatus detected in culture. Adenovirus-PCR positive.

round_3.working_diagnosis: AML with mutated TP53 (WHO 2022), ELN2022 adverse risk
differentials: Relapsed AML with TP53 mutation and clonal evolution
Secondary AML from MDS with complex karyotype

treatment_plan.action: Start azacytidine in combination with venetoclax, limiting venetoclax to 7-14 days per cycle due to infectious risk and cytopenias
Stop immunosuppression due to relapse and active infections
Order donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) from HLA-haploidentical brother
Evaluate eligibility for clinical trial of cellular therapy targeting CD123
Include palliative care and psycho-oncology team
Start liposomal amphotericin B for Aspergillus pneumonia (class switch from azole)
Add meropenem 1 g q8h for Pseudomonas aeruginosa superinfection
Start cidofovir 5 mg/kg i.v. weekly for 2 weeks then every 14 days for adenovirus infection with close renal monitoring

key_findings_quotes: Relapse of AML with clonal evolution, marked by emergence of an additional TP53 mutation (2025/01) and complex karyotype (2024/04).
The patient has failed multiple FLT3-inhibitor therapies.
Start quizartinib maintenance 26.5 mg daily. 2024-12-28: ECG showed QTc 512 ms and blood counts decreased
Relapse of AML with clonal evolution, accompanied by active bacterial & fungal pneumonia, adenovirus infection.
Final diagnosis: AML with mutated TP53 (WHO 2022), ELN2022 adverse.
